reviews.review_text: The budget family cabins are pretty grim. In cabin 2 the hot water only worked sometimes, the hot plates only worked sometimes, most power points didn't work, the mattresses were terrible. Absolutely tiny space - it was way too small for 2 adults and a child, let alone the 5 people it has capacity for. They really shouldn't be renting it out, it's not really fit for habitation. Disappointing because it's a nice spot.
Such a great place friendly staff well maintained we look forward to every visit
Lovely stay and great for anyone that enjoys mountain biking or hiking. Recommend to anyone travelling in the area.

Very cool campground. Not sure I’d recommend going over Easter, it felt over sold. Very hard to get a good spot, and nothing that felt like a ‘site’. Beware tent campers, there’s barely any level ground. Toilets and showers are outdated, don’t offer very good privacy, and not kept very clean.. that said, it’s a cool location with lots of biking, creek hangouts, hiking, good food options, and electric vehicle charging.

Great location, but they cram you in like sardines, even in the unpowered sites (rooms rating = campsites). Staff were friendly enough, but no partial refunds given for cutting the stay short. We moved to the Showgrounds anyway - far better campsites with space, but a bit further down the road.

Melrose Caravan Park is conveniently located at the foot of Mount Remarkable and within short walking distance of the small village.||Unfortunately, it was raining upon arrival and had clearly been quite wet for several days. ||Fortunately, the powered camp site was grassy and located within close (but not too close) proximity of the amenities. Ladies’ amenities have recently been renovated; very modern. Men’s amenities have yet to receive an upgrade, however are clean and tidy.||There’s a camp kitchen which appeared to be clean, tidy and well utilised.||We only stayed overnight and unfortunately the Mount Remarkable trails were closed - so no hikes. Can definitely recommend the North Star Hotel for dinner and a drink at the bar.
Lovely cabin with an easy walk to town in a beautiful bush setting.

The location of this caravan park is excellent, and the staff member on reception was professional and very knowledgeable about the local area. However, the camping experience for an unpowered camp-site was substandard due to the poor amenities and poor repair of the facilities in the back half of the campground. The toilets and showers for this area are a demountable unit that has seen hard use. Only one of the 5 toilet/ sink cubicles had both a working lock and a functional sink tap.
The shower units are poorly ventilated,so that as there were others showering at the same time, the unit was so steamy that I was just as hot and sweaty at the end of the shower as prior to taking it. The shower was leaking, so that it was not possible to get dressed after the shower without getting wet.
The camp kitchen appears fairly new, but one of the two stoves was not working and the taps in the sinks leak. There are flyscreens on the windows, but only snake wire above the windows, meaning that there were many insects in the camp kitchen.
We stayed for two nights and judging from the filling bins, lack of soap in the dispensers and no renewal of cleaning cloths, neither the toilet block or camp kitchen were cleaned in that time.
The amenities in the powered site section of the camp (400 m away by the road) were far superior, but also marred by lack on maintenance and repair, with a constantly running toilet and a broken sink tap in the women's amenities block.
The site itself was clean, but with no grass and a lot of rocks, which is to be expected for this location at this time of year. Many of the powered sites were grassed and the powered sites were flat.
Great little park. Super service, helpful with tips and advice. Superior access to mountain biking. Walking distance to Jackas Brewing.
What a fabulous place. Very clean and well appointed cabin. Office staff were friendly and helpful. We pulled in on a 42* day and the aircon had already been put on to cool the room before arrival.

The one star that I am giving in this review is only for the friendly and helpful lady working at reception.  Unfortunately the rest of the caravan park gets no stars at all. We have stayed here five times over the past few years and every stay has got progressively worse. Litter lying around the park, construction materials everywhere,  unlevel cramped and disjointed sites. The camp kitchen is uninviting, dirty and the oven out of order.
Worst of all are the bathrooms. They are dirty (they have definitely NOT been cleaned for a long time), they have a bad, permanent sewerage smell which has been like that since our first visit, taps, hinges, toilet seats and many other plumbing fixtures are broken and unmaintained.
Melrose is a beautiful town and we have continued to visit it as we like the mountain and supporting the local businesses,  but we have made the decision not to come back again. What a shame that poor management of a caravan park can have an impact on the local businesses.  Up your game G'day Melrose,  this is not acceptable.

Good park. Sites are very close together so if you don’t like being on top of someone else this is not the park for you. Didn’t use the amenities so can’t comment. Walking distance to the 2 local pubs and the service station/cafe/grocery store.
Caretakers were warm and very welcoming.
Very relaxing location with helpful staff.

This town and caravan park we liked so much we went all the way back there just to stay one night! While travelling across the Nullarbor from Perth WA to the Great Australian Bight, Barossa, wineries, Clare Valley, Flinders Ranges, Victor Harbor, Adelaide, then back through Esperance, and saw all the good South Australia has to offer over six weeks staying in really clean and friendly caravan parks including this one. Blew us away how clean and functional the whole tourism industry appears to be. Thank you for a fantastic stay. 10/10.
Lovely little stay while in Melrose, it has everything you need and the front desk is always helpful and informative
A rustic caravan park that gets the essentials right.
Just beautiful and peaceful

Stayed one night in a town I know well. This CP is at the foot of Mt Remarkable. Peaceful, lush at the moment following recent rain. Bush camping and higher end cabins. I stayed at the budget end...older but very clean, not musty. Heating, tv and peaceful. Staff are friendly and helpful.
For campers the camp kitchens are older but equipped with everything you need.

Receptionist was very nice, location is good, but would avoid due to the owner. When using the camp kitchen the owner came in and asked us if we were staying there - fine in itself, but then didn’t take yes for an answer? He proceeded to question if we had paid, if we had actually seen the girls at reception, if we were on unpowered, even after I said yes to each question. Found it very rude and he could have easily asked the reception first? He then also proceeded to give us a lecture on how clean the camp kitchen is and made us feel we shouldn’t be using it, even though we paid for it. Me and my partner are quiet, clean and respectful travellers and found this whole interaction rude, bordering on insulting, and I would avoid this location when passing through. Don’t own a caravan park if you don’t want people to use it.

Beautiful park spanning a wooded creek. Lots of room and variety. Great location in the centre of Melrose with walking and bike trails on the doorstep. Easy walking distance to the wonderful Jacka Brothers distillery and pubs in town.
BEEYOOSHIFUL, is my word du joir- great cabins, the VIEW! Oh my GOD it's amazing, there's hiking trails, bike tracks, playground for the kids - the service is through the roof - 10 out of 10!!
Just great, awesome location and clean facilities

This place is overrated if you’re planning a holiday. It could be great, but it’s definitely not, especially during school holidays. It’s incredibly noisy, with many visitors camping and drinking beers. They scream and laugh out loud until 11pm, completely disregarding families who want a quiet place to rest and enjoy nature. The lack of supervision and warnings from the owners makes the situation even worse. My little girl was almost hit twice by out-of-control teenage cyclists who were riding off the tracks. Another group of teenagers ran over the cord tensors of my tent three times because they thought it was a cool shortcut! It’s completely disrespectful. I didn’t enjoy my last visit as a camper. Hopefully it will change in the future for good.
Love it here, we come every year

Great location. Unfortunately the facilities in unpowered camping area were both inadequate, and unclean. Showers, but no washing facilities. They are the most poorly cleaned facilities we have seen in the month we have been travelling. If the site was full they would be overstretched. We have been to several GDay parks, and this is by far the poorest bathroom facilities. The camp kitchen is, however, very good and well equipped.
Nice park in a great location, particularly if interested in  MTB .
Excellent camping with family. Trail rides for all levels. Great location. Camp kitchen and toilets clean. Shower are small but reasonable water pressure. Locks need an upgrade, not all work.
Nice small park close to town.

Another Gem, we stayed early Sept 25, for 4 nights, the owners have really provided a great park, from basic cabins to 2 bedroom cabins, powered sites Loved our site 21, and very big free camping area. Amenities Clean well care for and a good bush kitchen. Can walk everywhere you need in town.
A gotta to stay there place.
Dog Friendly too👋
Great place to stay 👌

A great location within easy walking distance to Melrose township and the extensive walking and @mountainbike trails just across the the picturesque creek. This is a great location for @mounainbiking and there is a MTB outlet right in town Over the Edge Sports, with all the gear required to fix, maintain or purchase a MTB.

Absolutely stunning park.
Great customer service
Great friendly service . Cabins and facilities are clean but dated and could do with a refurb.
Loved the park, loved the brand new cabins and a short walk to a great pub
Very warm welcome. Lovely easy access site, near to creek. Water hot. Clean facilities. Thank you for making our first ever RV experience an easy and pleasant one.
The cabin accommodation was very comfortable. The Park is very well run and its so scenic. Great venue for 3 generations of our family.

What great little place to stay. Last minute check in with friends and they were so accommodating. They got us two powered sites side by side. Toilets and showers were clean and tidy and close by. Relaxed atmosphere and really easy walk to the swinging bridge and pub and the trails through the mountains are a real drawcard.
Lovely park. A mixture of powered and unpowered sites as well as cabins from very basic to luxury. Pet friendly and very helpful staff who even help you back your van into your spot

Stayed in this caravan park twice now and both times there has been mould all through the bathroom, floors aren’t swept let alone mopped and when you walk into the room you’re hit with the pungent smell of mould and so much glen 20 you can set off an asthma attack.
Issue was dismissed by staff saying “it’s been scrubbed”, sorry but the mould and fluff on the floors and top of the shower says otherwise.

Both times have left the caravan park feeling unwell and with a nasty cough. Won’t be coming back. You were given the benefit of the doubt after the first time but never again.
Beautiful setting
Stayed in a family deluxe villa. Very roomy and clean.
Lovely park nice and quiet

We went to the bush camp unpowered area which was great...pick your spot.
Amenities are average in this area but good hot showers.
Staff were amazing n helpful.
Dog friendly...with good walking areas. Very dusty...but no rain...
Very friendly owner who gave us good insights on what to do and where to go around the area. Female bathrooms clean and newly renovated, male bathrooms fine as well.

This is a hidden gem. Been here 3 times now mainly en route, but every time we are greeted like best friends. Super keen to make your stay memorable. Not a big park but that's not such a bad thing. Great clean amenities and huge camp kitchen.
Had an awesome time here. Cabin was excellent. The staff were super friendly. Site is at the base of Mt Remarkable and has quick and easy access to hikes and mountain bike trails. We'll be back.
What a gem of a spot. A lovely quiet caravan park. Lovely front office staff.
Always stay at the Caravan park when we travelling for work and always a pleasure.

This park is having a lot of work done to it. New high-end massive cabins (in the best camping spots) are nearing completion, tree and road maintenance work, etc. I can't give more than 2 stars because of the appalling conditions of the male amenities block. Apparently the female amenities have been renovated but the men's is in a dire state. One shower cubicle locked closed because it's all broken inside. One toilet locked closed because it's not working. They have terrible lighting and it just feels dirty, it hasn't been cleaned for a while by the looks. I hate to see van parks put all the energy and funds into cabins at the expense of traditional campers. I understand "that's where the big money is" in luxury cabins, but we certainly won't be back unless the amenities are renovated. It's quite disappointing. I'm also offended that men are apparently going to accept the state they are in, yet women wouldn't so they got an upgrade. Sad.
Great little quiet caravan park, beautiful location

Absolutely stunning park with brand new shower and toilet block which are lovely. Laundry block and clothes line with pegs available.
We stayed on a powered grass site with our camper which was right on the creek and it was just perfect for us. The kids were constantly over at the epic dirt bike jumps and across the road at the basketball court which had a playground right there.
There is a small outdoor kitchen with a hot plate and big BBQ. Everything is well taken care of and they are currently working on 3 massive cabin which will add to their other new ones.
Owners are lovely and so helpful, they lent us a few tools when we needed them!

Couldn’t recommend this park enough and it’s so close to Mt Remarkable and Alligator Gorge.

Great location and really good facilities on the site. I stayed in one of the cabins on the camp site and it was awesome, really comfy bed and great shower. The site has Wi-Fi which is good speed and easy to use and the reception is staffed by very friendly staff who are very knowledgeable about the local area and can recommend good walks near by and also where to eat in town. Thanks again for a great stay.

Great little caravan park ! Perfect spot for me and my partner to stay overnight while we made the most of the Melrose mountainbike trails.
The young lady who served us at reception was extremely accommodating and went above and beyond to ensure we had a great stay and left as happy as possible. Really want to thank her for that ! 😊 🤝

Receptionist working on 24/11 was absolutely lovely! Rooms are basic but super clean! I stayed in a double ensuite cabin on a Sunday night. Park was virtually empty. Everything you need. Only downside are the cockatoos.

Very clean and tidy.
Friendly and willing to help
Nice family owned place. Lose to cafes, pubs and nature trails.

I must say I was pretty disappointed at the state of this place. Clearly the owners do not care about cleanliness or hygiene. It wouldn't take much to employ someone for a couple of hours a day to clean the amenities. When we got there, I went to use the toilet, it was filthy. I went to the same toilet the next day, still just as gross, and the next day. So in 3 days, no one had come to clean them, or even replace the toilet paper.... Camp kitchen needed a thorough wipe down as everything in it was covered in grime and dust... Showers, toilets, sinks, pretty much everything is left to the people staying to clean. One of the dirtiest and grossest parks I've stayed at, and it wasn't even that busy so there is really no excuse for the filthy state it was in...

We walked through as we walked to the monument in the Conservation Park. It is a beautiful short walk. The caravan/camping area was packed due to the long weekend it looks like bush camping sites, but quite lovely.

The plots may not be the biggest but they make you closer to your neighbours, which is good for a chat. Bring a TV cable to plug into the point provided. The campsite is brilliant for easy walks to the two local pubs and the 'bike shop bistro',  and the fabulous brewery (did someone say *brewery*...yes...hic! - and it does pizzas😋) Don't forget the local museum. You can also have a fire pit at your campsite, just purchase a bag of wood at the site.

This is a very quiet and amazing place. Kids love the bike tracks. Our family has a tradition of coming each year together as this is a friendly place. Just one tiny suggestion; can the office please get some lollies as that I tried to get some mint this morning and I had to go down to the servo.|Quite good, and I strongly recommend coming

Powered and unpowered campsites. There are some good camp kitchens. There are bathrooms on both sides of the creek but the ones near the reception building are definitely better.
Very popular for mountain bikers.
Campfires are allowed if it's not fire ban season
Lots of families with kids when we stayed
Great position close to everything Melrose has to offer, but the unpowered campground is tired ie random piles of ash everywhere, and the amenities block was in need of a good clean.

Beautiful setting at the base of Mt Remarkable.  Large shady gum trees & relaxed atmosphere. Lots of walking & bike trails to enjoy. From the moment we arrived, I was greeted with a big friendly smile & chat with the owner on check in.
Clean updated amenities.
Short walking distance to Jacka Brewing for wood fired pizza & drinks, Museum & 2 Pubs. Can't recommend highly enough after spending 6 days here 🌞
We have enjoyed stays in our caravan at this Park on several occasions. Jules and Mark are very professional and friendly.
Great  park , bush setting with 2 pubs almost  beside  it, doesn't  get much better , 8th
Prime location for camping. Great place to access the huge trail network on the slopes of Mount Remarkable.
Good park average showers and  a great pub down the road
Great place to stay with kids. Close to MTB, walking and running trails. Can get very busy and crowded over long weekends so book in advance for these.

Hiking the Heysen Trail, we dropped supplies here, and stayed overnight on our way north. They kindly accommodated our last minute request to upgrade to a cabin. At all times the staff were obliging and friendly, giving us useful advice and helping us on our way. Thanks team.
Was a bit disappointed in the customer service when I had to cancel last minute due to vehicle malfunctions - was quite rude over the phone with no ability to offer a refund.
In one of the most beautiful part's of south Australia well worth the stop and have a look around while there
Powered site

A very picturesque caravan park at the foot of Mt Remarkable. The sites are a good size, the camp kitchen was very spacious, clean and well equipped and the amenities block was fairly new. Fire pits were provided for each gully site. The staff were very friendly and helpful.

Great oasis, perfect spot for epic bike trails beginning.
Really nice owners
Excellent Allround

I’ve stayed here previously but after coming from the campsite at Wilpena Pound Resort, $21 per person for an unpowered campsite felt steep. I know that this caravan park gets a huge workout as Melrose is growing in popularity. But the showers and toilets are portable and feel tired. The kitchen doesn’t feel clean or fresh. Will be back because location is unreal but there’s definitely some worthy upgrades to be had.

We were tenting non powered.|Lousy small showers with no bench to put your stuff on.|I was stark naked only to find my hot water Tap did not work.|Nothing worse than having to go to the next cubicle in the nude|Kitchen alright but no lights to turn on when it got dark.|Definitely not as good as the Rawsley park station facility

Extremely helpful and informative despite not being paying customers. A+ for friendliness and I would definitely consider staying if I was in the market for accommodation. The cabins look good from what we saw.

**PET FRIENDLY CABINS**
We stayed with 3 dogs at short notice. The staff were very friendly and helpful, giving us some local tips. The rooms are very clean and well equipped with a full kitchen set up, which was a bargain at $135 a night. It has also been adapted to make it mobility friendly and accessible. Would stay again.
Super friendly hosts. Perfect spot for exploring the mountain bike trails. Thanks for making this adventure happen for us.
Great spot to explore

Lovely park we had a family stay 5 powered sites and a cabin for new years and it was fantastic. The owner was very accommodating to our young pup as was the local pubs and town . Recommend a stay here
Great overnight stop
Awesome place to stay at also the meals at the Mt Remarkable opposite the park were so tasty and well prepared.
It was a 42 degree day and too hot to set up camp so we were very grateful that Melrose Caravan park is pet friendly and had a spare cabin. Thank you that you fitted us in. Rgds Cathy
We were very comfortable on a powered, flat site. Cabins available.  Unpowered bush sites across the creek were very inviting.  We will be back!

Just an overnight stop without a booking. Owner was happy to accommodate my van and recommended a tyre repairer who was also very accommodating to fix my puncture. Good facilities in the park, a bit dated, but that's a good thing. Shower curtains would stop water splashing into the changing section of shower cubicles. The very noisy, I assume, exhaust system of the ablutions block seems to switch off at 11pm which provides relief of the noise at night. Highly recommended park.
Lovely, clean  cabin and a nice location.
Absolutely fabulous park and recently got new mangers, they are fabulous  and are improving the park
Favourite place for easy bush camping (with facilities)

Beautiful place the website does not do it justice. Trevor is amazing and so kind to our young family. Will definitely visit again and recommend to everyone. Only thing I’d recommend is wifi so kids can watch a movie one night
Friendly country hospitality and plenty of services

We have been to this park 3 times in the last year and loved it every time. The location and grounds are fabulous for camping on an unpowered site under beautiful big old trees full of birds. We were able to purchase wood for a lovely campfire under the stars and used their great laundry.  The only thing that would make this park better is if they cleaned the toilets twice a day when school groups are present as they tend to get pretty grotty with the high  useage. Thanks for a great park. We love it.

Pleasant setting by the river and a fair bit to do around the place.  Several good walking tracks - including part of Heysen trail.  Town also has some places of interest.  Caravan Park well set up and clean.

Beautiful park at the foot of Mount Remarkable. Friendly owners and the cabin was spotless.  Shower water was only warmish but did the job.
Heaps of caravan sites nestled in amongst the trees and an excellent camping site across the creek. Gateway to all the mountain bike riding and walking trails.
The caravan park is at the bottom of Mount Remarkable, clean and easy to use facilities and lovely owners.

Unpowered area is big, but if you are planning camping when there is an event in Melrose, make sure you arrive early. We almost didn’t get a spot for camping even booking it in advance.
This was the most noisy camping I have ever been. People were making noisy up to 4 am.
Amenities are terrible for unpowered sites. I happened to go to female toilets at the powered sites side early in the morning and was surprised with a woman and man entering the female toilet to shower together. No respect at all. It happened too early (7am ish) to contact someone from the office. For long weekend would be great if the office and staffs are available earlier and check the noisy at night. I do not recommend this place.

Beautiful little spot in the heart of town. Quiet. A warm welcome on arrival, and very accommodating. A short walk to the fabulous Melrose Hotel for a delicious meal. A new family I believe, doing an excellent job! Thank you.

Melrose has been one of our last stops when coming back from holidays as we always enjoy our stay, our recent stay in the unpowered site area was somewhat disappointing. Two of the showers were faulty with no signage to notify, had to swap into another, floors and shower were dirty, toilet bins full and paper on the floor, tap at basin not working properly. There is a great camp kitchen which we used, there needs to be a bit more attention to cleaning around hot plates, I cleaned one with ease. It would be a shame if the cleanliness of this park effects their business.
We stayed a couple of nights here and enjoyed the experience. Nice walks close by and good facilities.
